purpose: OS-installer-style preset+override selector for env-install pipeline. Inspired by Debian tasksel + Red Hat Anaconda. Consumed by `zorbit env install` (zorbit-cli) and `bootstrap-env.sh`.
design_notes: Modules listed here MUST resolve to entries in zorbit-core/platform-spec/all-repos.yaml.
Modules absent from all-repos.yaml are flagged in the `planned_modules` section — installer will warn + skip them until they are published.
Preset selection is layered: (a) base preset, (b) --add modules, (c) --remove modules, (d) write resolved lockfile to /etc/zorbit/<env>-install.lock.
Memory + disk estimates are coarse — derived from observed footprint of zorbit-dev (16 GB VM with 66 services consumed ~12 GB RAM).

core_platform_services.label: Core Platform Services
core_platform_services.description: Identity, authorization, navigation, audit, event bus, PII vault, observability, secrets vault, module registry, deployment registry. Always required for any working env.

developer_center.label: Developer Center
developer_center.description: ZMB factory, observability dashboards, deployment registry tooling — for platform engineers.

platform_capabilities.label: Platform Capabilities (PFS)
platform_capabilities.description: Reusable platform feature services — data tables, forms, chat, AI gateway, doc generator, etc.

agentic_calling.label: Agentic Calling
agentic_calling.description: AI-powered telephony / underwriting calls. Includes Jayna agentic calling module.

user_profile_preferences.label: User & Tenant Settings
user_profile_preferences.description: User profile, white-label theme/branding, edition selector.

business_health_insurance.label: Business — Health Insurance
business_health_insurance.description: Insurance domain modules — quotation, underwriting, claims, decisioning, network empanelment, broker, customer portal.

third_party_modules.label: Third-Party Modules (TPM)
third_party_modules.description: Integrated third-party systems — Jitsi (video conferencing), Odoo (ERP). Mapped via TPM containers, not Zorbit-built.

shared_infrastructure.label: Shared Infrastructure (zs-*)
shared_infrastructure.description: Shared infra containers — Postgres, MongoDB, Kafka, Redis, Superset. Always provisioned regardless of preset (unless --skip-shared-infra).

minimal.label: Minimal — bare platform skeleton
minimal.description: Just enough to login + see an empty sidebar. Core platform services + shared infra + unified console only. Use for: dev sandbox, debugging, smoke tests.

recommended.label: Recommended — demo-ready
recommended.description: Core + key PFS + 3 demo business modules. Enough to show the platform in action with a couple of live ZMBs. Use for: QA env, demos, integration testing.

max.label: Max — full platform parity
max.description: Every published service in every group. Mirrors prod feature surface. Use for: prod-mirror staging, full feature regression. Memory hungry — needs 12+ GB.

developer.label: Developer — minimal + dev tools
developer.description: Minimal preset plus the developer center (ZMB factory, observability, deployment registry). Use for: building new modules.

validation_rules.module_must_exist_in_manifest: Every module in `groups.*.modules` MUST resolve to an entry in zorbit-core/platform-spec/all-repos.yaml. The resolver will fail-fast if any does not.
validation_rules.planned_modules_skipped: Entries under `groups.*.planned_modules` are documented future work — the resolver will WARN and skip them at install time.
validation_rules.always_required_groups: Groups marked `always_required: true` are merged into every preset, even if not explicitly listed in `includes_groups`.
validation_rules.cli_override_grammar: --add and --remove accept comma-separated module slugs (with or without `zorbit-` prefix). Resolver normalises to canonical slugs from all-repos.yaml.
